fundamentals.name: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. engages in the design, manufacture, and sale of fiber-optic networking products in the United States, Taiwan, and China. The company offers optical modules, optical filters, lasers, laser components, subassemblies, transmitters and transceivers, turn-key equipment, headend, node, and distribution equipment, as well as amplifiers. It sells its products to internet data center operators, cable television, telecom equipment manufacturers, fiber-to-the-home, and internet service providers through its direct and indirect sales channels. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Sugar Land, Texas.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing company with high valuation and operational losses, balanced by strong revenue growth and analyst optimism.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 51.4% YoY, with analyst estimates projecting 441.7% for the current fiscal year.
Forward P/E of 36.12 and PEG ratio of 0.78 suggest growth may justify the valuation.
Strong current and quick ratios (3.83 and 2.85) indicate good short-term liquidity.
Low debt relative to equity (25.36%) and cash exceeding debt ($439.70M vs $280.42M) provide a stable balance sheet.
negatives: Operating and profit margins are negative (-8.6% and -8.5% respectively), indicating the company is not yet profitable.
Return on equity and assets are also negative (-6.1% and -3.3%), showing poor efficiency in generating profits.
The stock has experienced extreme volatility, with a beta of 3.76 and a 635.5% price increase in six months.
Last quarter's EPS missed estimates by 44.8%.

news.paragraph: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) has seen significant positive attention recently, with articles highlighting substantial transceiver orders and upward revisions to analyst targets. Despite a notable recent price surge and questions about its valuation, the narrative remains bullish due to strong revenue growth and analyst estimates pointing to massive earnings growth. The company's operational losses and high debt-to-equity ratio present risks, but the market appears focused on the growth trajectory.
